headline: Jim Cramer on Dollar General: “We See If It’s as Good as Dollar Tree”
summary: Dollar General Corporation (NYSE:DG) was among the stocks Jim Cramer was focused on, as he discussed Mad Money’s latest game plan for the week. With the company reporting on Tuesday, Cramer expects DG to report earnings similar to Dollar Tree’s. The Mad Money host said: Now that same morning, we see Dollar General. We see […]

headline: Dollar Tree (DLTR) Is Up 22.6% After Raising Guidance And Expanding Its Value Convenience Strategy – Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: In late May 2026, Dollar Tree reported first-quarter revenue of about US$4.98 billion and net income of US$347.3 million, alongside guidance for full-year net sales of US$20.5 billion to US$20.7 billion and plans for roughly 400 new store openings and 75 closures in 2026. Management also highlighted expanding multi-price assortments, margin improvements, a new DoorDash partnership spanning more than 9,000 stores, and an ongoing share repurchase program that has retired about 30% of shares...

headline: Assessing Dollar Tree (DLTR) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
summary: Why Dollar Tree stock is drawing fresh attention Dollar Tree (DLTR) is back on many investors’ watchlists after recent share price moves, with the stock up around 23% over the past month and almost flat over the past 3 months. See our latest analysis for Dollar Tree. The recent 23% 1 month share price return, alongside a 22.6% gain over the last week, contrasts with a slightly weaker 3 month share price return and mixed multi year total shareholder returns. This suggests that short term...

headline: DLTR Q1 Deep Dive: Margin Gains and Assortment Expansion Amid Consumer Caution
summary: Discount treasure-hunt retailer Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 7.2% year on year to $4.98 billion. The company expects next quarter’s revenue to be around $4.85 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.74 per share was 12.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
